body_txt: For many people, a job is normally just a salary to pay the bills and put a roof over their head, but others dream of getting their dream job, where they will love working, achieve their goals, and get paid for enjoying it. Some are able to land this kind of job: top novelists, filmmakers, and actors. But there can be a fine line between a dream come true... and a complete nightmare. The Jaded Professional is someone who used to love their job, but after working for a long time there, they started resenting it, if not outright hating it. Maybe the paperwork and rules of the Vast Bureaucracy destroys the joy of the job, or maybe the character themselves hadn't thought about any of the downsides (long hours, rabid fans, no time for relaxation). One example is politicians and government officials, where some people start doing it to make a change, but after a while, they learn that the Obstructive Bureaucrat and Amoral Attorney will block them at every turn until they stop trying. Another example is the top actor or movie director who at first loves the creativity, wealth, and attention, but by mid-career, they are bitter about Executive Meddling in their projects and frustrated by intrusive paparazzi and fans. This character tends to be an antagonist and/or mentor for the Naïve Newcomer , they may try to convince them that they should just give up trying to enter the profession. In the most idealistic works, the resolve of the protagonist will help them find joy in their job again. Normally a deconstruction of Pursue the Dream Job . Sub-Trope of Jade-Colored Glasses and Super-Trope of Hates the Job, Loves the Limelight and Apathetic Teacher . Compare Trade Your Passion for Glory and Shell-Shocked Veteran .
